docs.title: HY Credit Screener — Field Reference
docs.text:
This is the field reference for the high-yield European corporate-bond credit screener, rebuilt on free data (yfinance) to replace the original Bloomberg dependencies.

Every issuer-level field is documented here: what it is, its exact formula, where each input is pulled from and how it is transformed, the proxies and caveats, a simplified code sketch, and a reconciliation note against the calibration name (Ball Corp, BALL).

docs.title: The one rule that shapes everything
docs.text:
For every field we report the metric exactly as the Excel defines it, at two points:

  • latest — the newest available value (TTM rolled to the issuer's reporting cadence, the latest balance-sheet level, or the latest market value).
  • fy_baseline — the same metric as of the last annual statement. This is the guaranteed floor: it always exists, even for annual-only reporters.

Each carries its exact as_of date. Derived fields additionally expose their components (the in-between values) at both points, plus a period_consistent flag that fires when the components' dates don't line up.

docs.title: Running it
docs.text:
import yfinance as yf
exec(open('screener.py').read())

api = yf.Ticker('BALL')
res = inspect(api)            # prints every field: latest + FY + component breakdown
all_fields = build_all(api)   # dict {field_name: contract}

See The field contract for the output shape and The engine for date / frequency handling.

docs.title: The field contract
docs.text:
Every getter returns the same shape, so fields are interchangeable downstream.

{
  'field': 'fcf',
  'unit':  'mm',                 # mm | x | decimal
  'basis': 'derived',            # ttm | level | market | derived | stat
  'latest':      {'value': ..., 'as_of': 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'basis': ...},
  'fy_baseline': {'value': ..., 'as_of': 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'fy': 2025, 'basis': ...},
  'source':  'Yahoo Finance · ...',
  'formula': 'fcf = ebitda + capex + ch_nwc + cash_interest + cash_taxes',
  'desc':    '...',
  'is_proxy': True
}

docs.title: The two headline points
docs.text:
point meaning comes from latest metric as the Excel defines it, newest available TTM roll / latest level / latest market fy_baseline same metric as of the last annual statement (the floor) the annual statement only

fy_baseline is always sourced from the annual statement, never a sum of interim periods — that is what makes it the guaranteed floor for every ticker, including annual-only reporters.

docs.title: Basis types
docs.text:
• ttm — a flow, rolled to cover 12 months (4 quarters or 2 half-years).
  • level — a balance-sheet stock, taken at the latest snapshot.
  • market — price-derived, valued on price dates.
  • derived — computed from other fields at both points.
  • stat — a multi-year dispersion statistic (only ufcf_vol); no latest/FY pair.

docs.title: Derived fields: components & consistency
docs.text:
Derived fields are evaluated twice — once from each component's latest, once from each component's fy_baseline — and expose the in-between values:

res['fcf']['latest']['components']
# {'ebitda': {'value': 2110, 'date': '2026-03-31'},
#  'capex':  {'value': -554, 'date': '2026-03-31'}, ... }

res['fcf']['latest']['period_consistent']   # True if all components share one date

period_consistent=False is not an error — it flags an unavoidable date mismatch, e.g. a market equity_cap (price date) sitting next to balance-sheet levels in net_debt_to_ev.

docs.title: Proxies
docs.text: is_proxy=True marks a field that relies on an approximation (e.g. accrual interest standing in for cash interest). The flag propagates: any derived field built on a proxy inherits it.

docs.title: The engine
docs.text: The engine handles dates and reporting cadence so the field getters don't have to.

docs.title: Frequency detection
docs.text:
infer_frequency() reads the spacing of the interim statements and classifies the issuer:

cadence periods/yr (ppy) TTM roll quarterly 4 sum of last 4 semi-annual 2 sum of last 2 annual-only 1 no TTM (FY value only)

European semi-annual reporters

For many EU issuers the half-year statements are filed under yfinance's quarterly_* attributes (quarterly_income_stmt, etc.) — not a separate semi-annual frame. The engine reads quarterly_*, measures the ~6-month spacing, and detects semiannual automatically, so a TTM rolls 2 halves rather than 4 quarters.

docs.title: TTM rolling
docs.text: A ttm flow is the sum of the last ppy interim periods, dated at the newest interim. We roll our own from interim statements rather than using Yahoo's ttm_* line, because rolling is uniform, dated, and semi-annual-capable.

docs.title: The FY-start floor
docs.text: fy_baseline always comes from the annual statement. Because the annual figure exists for every issuer, every field is guaranteed to return at least its last-annual value — the floor — even when interim data is missing.

docs.title: Fiscal-year inference
docs.text: The fiscal year-end month is inferred per issuer from the annual statement dates (not assumed to be December), which drives the FY boundaries used by interpolated fields such as ltm_vs_2y_avg_ebitda.

docs.title: Adjusted EBITDA
docs.text:
adj_ebitda

At a glance

Basis: ttm  ·  Unit: mm

Trailing-twelve-month normalized EBITDA — Yahoo's figure with one-off / unusual items stripped out.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
adj_ebitda = TTM rolling of income_stmt['Normalized EBITDA']

Reported at two points — latest (Σ of the last 4Q / 2H of Normalized EBITDA) and FY-baseline (the last annual Normalized EBITDA), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Normalized EBITDA income_stmt['Normalized EBITDA'] TTM-rolled +

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return resolve_field(api, 'adj_ebitda')

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball LTM ≈ 2,086, FY2025 = 2,042 (close to reported — few adjustments).

docs.title: Capital Expenditure
docs.text:
capex

At a glance

Basis: ttm  ·  Unit: mm

Trailing-twelve-month capex. Stored negative (a cash outflow), exactly as Yahoo reports it, so it sums straight into the cash-flow fields.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
capex = TTM rolling of cash_flow['Capital Expenditure Reported']   (fallback 'Capital Expenditure')

Reported at two points — latest (Σ of the last 4Q / 2H, kept negative) and FY-baseline (the last annual capex), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Capital Expenditure Reported cash_flow['Capital Expenditure Reported'] TTM-rolled, sign kept negative − (outflow)

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: If Capital Expenditure Reported is absent the generic Capital Expenditure line is used (first present wins).

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return resolve_field(api, 'capex')

docs.title: Cash & Equivalents
docs.text:
cash

At a glance

Basis: level  ·  Unit: mm

Cash, equivalents and short-term investments — a balance-sheet level, taken at the latest snapshot (not summed).

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
cash = balance_sheet['Cash Cash Equivalents And Short Term Investments']   (fallback 'Cash And Cash Equivalents')

Reported at two points — latest (the newest interim balance-sheet snapshot) and FY-baseline (the FY-end snapshot), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Cash line balance_sheet['Cash Cash Equivalents And Short Term Investments'] level (latest snapshot) +

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return resolve_field(api, 'cash')

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball latest ≈ 730, FY2025 = 1,212.

docs.title: Cash Interest
docs.text:
cash_interest

At a glance

Basis: ttm  ·  Unit: mm  ·  Proxy: ⚠️ yes

Trailing-twelve-month cash interest paid. Stored negative. Yahoo exposes no cash-paid-for-interest line, so the accrual interest expense is used as a proxy.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
cash_interest = -(TTM rolling of income_stmt['Interest Expense'])

Reported at two points — latest (−Σ of the last 4Q / 2H of Interest Expense) and FY-baseline (−(last annual Interest Expense)), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Interest Expense income_stmt['Interest Expense'] TTM-rolled, negated − (outflow)

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Proxy. Accrual interest runs above true cash interest (it includes non-cash OID / issuance-cost amortization and timing). Flagged is_proxy=True; any field built on it inherits the flag.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return resolve_field(api, 'cash_interest')   # sign=-1 applied in the registry

docs.title: Cash Taxes
docs.text:
cash_taxes

At a glance

Basis: ttm  ·  Unit: mm  ·  Proxy: ⚠️ yes

Trailing-twelve-month cash taxes paid. Stored negative. Approximated by the current tax expense (total provision less the non-cash deferred piece).

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
cash_taxes = -(TTM Tax Provision - TTM Deferred Tax)     (fallback: -(TTM Tax Provision))

Reported at two points — latest (−(TTM Tax Provision − TTM Deferred Tax)) and FY-baseline (−(annual Tax Provision − annual Deferred Tax)), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Tax Provision income_stmt['Tax Provision'] TTM-rolled (total tax) + before negation Deferred Tax cash_flow['Deferred Tax'] (fallback Deferred Income Tax) TTM-rolled, subtracted to leave current tax −

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Proxy. Still omits the change in taxes-payable that only the 10-K 'cash paid for income taxes' line captures (Yahoo lacks it). If no deferred line exists, falls back to total provision.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
prov = TTM('Tax Provision');  deferred = TTM('Deferred Tax')
return -(prov - deferred)   # negated; current-tax proxy

docs.title: Change in Net Working Capital
docs.text:
ch_nwc

At a glance

Basis: ttm  ·  Unit: mm

Trailing-twelve-month change in net working capital, cash-signed (negative = cash consumed by working capital). Resolved through a three-tier ladder, first that yields a value wins.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
Tier 1: ch_nwc = TTM rolling of cash_flow['Change In Working Capital']
Tier 2: ch_nwc = TTM rolling of Σ(cash-flow working-capital component lines)
Tier 3: ch_nwc = -[(AR+Inventory-AP)_t - (AR+Inventory-AP)_{t-1}]   (balance-sheet proxy)

Reported at two points — latest (Σ of the last 4Q / 2H of the resolved tier) and FY-baseline (the last annual value of the resolved tier), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Change In Working Capital cash_flow['Change In Working Capital'] TTM-rolled, cash-signed ± WC components (Tier 2) cash_flow Δ receivables/inventory/payables/other summed, then TTM-rolled ± AR / Inventory / AP (Tier 3) balance_sheet year-on-year change of (AR+Inv−AP), negated ±

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: It is a cash-flow concept, not a balance-sheet level difference. Tier 3 uses a different (balance-sheet) basis and can flip sign on some names — it carries is_proxy=True.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
# Tier 1 (almost always available):
q = quarterly_cash_flow['Change In Working Capital']
return resolve_flow('ch_nwc', q, annual_equivalent)

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball LTM ≈ −395 (rolling 4Q), FY2025 = −131.

docs.title: EBIT
docs.text:
ebit

At a glance

Basis: ttm  ·  Unit: mm

Trailing-twelve-month operating profit. Used to size the unlevered tax in ufcf.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
ebit = TTM income_stmt['EBIT']   (fallback: TTM(EBITDA) - TTM(Reconciled Depreciation))

Reported at two points — latest (Σ of the last 4Q / 2H of EBIT) and FY-baseline (the last annual EBIT), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign EBIT income_stmt['EBIT'] TTM-rolled + EBITDA − Reconciled Depreciation (fallback) income_stmt TTM-rolled difference +

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return resolve_field-style: TTM('EBIT')  else  TTM('EBITDA') - TTM('Reconciled Depreciation')

docs.title: EBITDA
docs.text:
ebitda

At a glance

Basis: ttm  ·  Unit: mm

Trailing-twelve-month reported EBITDA. The reported (not adjusted) figure; the adjusted variant is adj_ebitda.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
ebitda = TTM rolling of income_stmt['EBITDA']

Reported at two points — latest (Σ of the last 4Q / 2H of EBITDA) and FY-baseline (the last annual EBITDA), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign EBITDA income_stmt['EBITDA'] TTM-rolled +

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return resolve_field(api, 'ebitda')

docs.title: (EBITDA − Capex) / Interest
docs.text:
ebitda_less_capex_to_interest

At a glance

Basis: derived  ·  Unit: x  ·  Proxy: ⚠️ yes

Coverage multiple: cash earnings after capex relative to the cash cost of interest (Excel AE6).

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
ebitda_less_capex_to_interest = (ebitda + capex) / -cash_interest

Reported at two points — latest (ratio at each component's latest) and FY-baseline (ratio at each component's FY value), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign ebitda get_ebitda TTM + capex get_capex TTM, negative (so it reduces the numerator) + cash_interest get_cash_interest TTM, negated again in the denominator denominator

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Inherits the cash_interest accrual proxy in the denominator, which amplifies that gap. is_proxy=True.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: m = (v['ebitda'] + v['capex']) / (-v['cash_interest'])

docs.title: EBITDA / Adjusted EBITDA
docs.text:
ebitda_to_adj_ebitda

At a glance

Basis: derived  ·  Unit: x

Ratio of reported to adjusted EBITDA — how much 'adjustment' is in the headline number. Near 1.0 means few add-backs.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
ebitda_to_adj_ebitda = ebitda / adj_ebitda

Reported at two points — latest (ratio of each component's latest) and FY-baseline (ratio of each component's FY value), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign ebitda get_ebitda TTM numerator adj_ebitda get_adj_ebitda TTM denominator

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball ≈ 1.01 (minimal adjustments).

docs.title: Equity Market Cap
docs.text:
equity_cap

At a glance

Basis: market  ·  Unit: mm

Market capitalisation = outstanding shares × share price. A market field, so it is valued on price dates, not statement dates.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
equity_cap = shares_outstanding × share_price

Reported at two points — latest (latest outstanding shares × latest close) and FY-baseline (FY-end outstanding shares × close on the FY-end date), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Ordinary Shares Number balance_sheet['Ordinary Shares Number'] level; outstanding (not 'Share Issued') + Close history(auto_adjust=True)['Close'], tz-stripped latest close / close on FY-end date +

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Outstanding shares are the right base for market cap (issued ⊇ outstanding). When equity_cap feeds a ratio its date is the price date, which is why net_debt_to_ev can report period_consistent=False.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
shares = balance_sheet['Ordinary Shares Number']
price  = history()['Close']
latest = shares.iloc[-1] * price.iloc[-1]
fy     = shares_at_fy_end * close_on(fy_end_date)

docs.title: Free Cash Flow
docs.text:
fcf

At a glance

Basis: derived  ·  Unit: mm  ·  Proxy: ⚠️ yes

Levered free cash flow — cash after capex, working-capital swings, and the cash cost of interest and tax. Computed from components (Excel Y6 = SUM(S6:X6)), not Yahoo's opaque Free Cash Flow line.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
fcf = ebitda + capex + ch_nwc + cash_interest + cash_taxes   (all cash-signed → straight sum)

Reported at two points — latest (each component at its latest TTM value) and FY-baseline (each component at its FY-start value), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign ebitda get_ebitda TTM + capex get_capex TTM, negative − ch_nwc get_ch_nwc TTM, cash-signed ± cash_interest get_cash_interest TTM, negated (proxy) − cash_taxes get_cash_taxes TTM current-tax (proxy) −

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: cash_interest and cash_taxes are accrual proxies, so fcf carries is_proxy=True.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
parts = [get_ebitda, get_capex, get_ch_nwc, get_cash_interest, get_cash_taxes]
latest = sum(p(api)['latest']['value']      for p in parts)
fy     = sum(p(api)['fy_baseline']['value'] for p in parts)

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball LTM ≈ 678 vs Bloomberg 735 (~8% under; component errors partly offset). Using Bloomberg's annual capex instead gives ≈758.

docs.title: FCF / Net Debt
docs.text:
fcf_to_net_debt

At a glance

Basis: derived  ·  Unit: decimal  ·  Proxy: ⚠️ yes

Deleveraging capacity: how much of net debt the levered FCF covers in a year.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
fcf_to_net_debt = fcf / (total_debt - cash)

Reported at two points — latest (TTM FCF over latest net-debt levels) and FY-baseline (FY FCF over FY-end net-debt levels), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign fcf get_fcf TTM levered (proxy) numerator total_debt get_total_debt level denominator cash get_cash level denominator

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Inherits fcf's interest/tax proxies → is_proxy=True.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: ratio = v['fcf'] / (v['total_debt'] - v['cash'])

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball latest ≈ 0.10, FY2025 ≈ 0.17.

docs.title: Implied Equity Vol (1y)
docs.text:
implied_vol

At a glance

Basis: market  ·  Unit: decimal

One-year implied equity volatility — parked. Returns None.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
implied_vol → None   (substitute realized_vol)

Reported at two points — latest (None) and FY-baseline (None), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Yahoo option IV option chains unusable for this universe —

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Yahoo's impliedVolatility is unreliable here (illiquid chains, solver bisection artifacts). The field is kept for completeness; the model substitutes realized_vol.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return {'latest': None, 'fy_baseline': None}   # parked

docs.title: LTM EBITDA / 2-Year Average
docs.text:
ltm_vs_2y_avg_ebitda

At a glance

Basis: derived  ·  Unit: x

Earnings-momentum smoother: LTM EBITDA against its trailing two-year average. The two points use deliberately different averages.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
ratio = ltm_ebitda / two_year_avg
LATEST two_year_avg = [ YTD(current FY) + FY-1(full) + ((ppy-n)/ppy)·FY-2(full) ] / 2
FY     two_year_avg = mean(last 2 annual EBITDA)

Reported at two points — latest (interpolated trailing-24-month average (ppy = 4 quarterly / 2 semi-annual; n = current interim index)) and FY-baseline (simple mean of the last two annual statements), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign ltm_ebitda get_ebitda latest TTM numerator interim EBITDA quarterly_income_stmt['EBITDA'] (EU halves live here too) YTD of current FY 24m build annual EBITDA income_stmt['EBITDA'] FY-1 full + fractional FY-2 tail 24m build

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: The interpolation reconstructs exactly 24 months from annuals + current-FY interims, because Yahoo rarely exposes 8 interim periods. At a fiscal year-end the interpolated value collapses to the annual mean, as it should; mid-year they diverge.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
ytd = Σ current-FY interim EBITDA up to latest date
two_y_latest = (ytd + annual[fy-1] + ((ppy-n)/ppy)*annual[fy-2]) / 2
two_y_fy     = mean(annual[-2:])

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball ≈ 1.2 (LTM running above the 2-year average).

docs.title: Net Debt / EBITDA
docs.text:
net_debt_to_ebitda

At a glance

Basis: derived  ·  Unit: x

Headline leverage. A balance-sheet level numerator over a TTM flow denominator.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
net_debt_to_ebitda = (total_debt - cash) / ebitda

Reported at two points — latest (levels at the newest balance-sheet date over TTM EBITDA) and FY-baseline (FY-end levels over annual EBITDA), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign total_debt get_total_debt level numerator cash get_cash level numerator ebitda get_ebitda TTM denominator

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: period_consistent flags when the level date (balance sheet) and the TTM date (latest interim) differ.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
nd = v['total_debt'] - v['cash']
ratio = nd / v['ebitda']

docs.title: Net Debt / Enterprise Value
docs.text:
net_debt_to_ev

At a glance

Basis: derived  ·  Unit: decimal

Leverage relative to enterprise value — the share of EV funded by net debt.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
net_debt_to_ev = (total_debt - cash) / (equity_cap + total_debt - cash)

Reported at two points — latest (market equity_cap (price date) + net-debt levels) and FY-baseline (FY-end equity_cap + FY-end net-debt levels), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign total_debt get_total_debt level net debt cash get_cash level net debt equity_cap get_equity_cap market (shares × price) EV

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: equity_cap is on a price date while debt/cash are on the balance-sheet date, so period_consistent is typically False here — correctly flagging the unavoidable date mismatch.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
nd = v['total_debt'] - v['cash']
ev = v['equity_cap'] + nd
ratio = nd / ev

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball latest ≈ 0.33.

docs.title: Realized Equity Vol (1y)
docs.text:
realized_vol

At a glance

Basis: market  ·  Unit: decimal

Annualised standard deviation of daily log equity returns. The structural model uses this in place of implied vol.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
realized_vol = stdev(ln(Close_t / Close_{t-1})) × sqrt(252)

Reported at two points — latest (trailing 252 trading days up to the latest close) and FY-baseline (the last full fiscal year's daily log returns), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Close history(auto_adjust=True)['Close'], tz-stripped daily log returns; std × √252 —

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Unit is a decimal (0.26 = 26%). The FY value uses the calendar/fiscal-year window the Excel/BVAL basis assumes.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
lr = np.log(close / close.shift(1)).dropna()
latest = lr.iloc[-252:].std() * np.sqrt(252)

docs.title: Revenues
docs.text:
revenues

At a glance

Basis: ttm  ·  Unit: mm

Trailing-twelve-month total revenue — the top line, rolled to cover the last 12 months on whatever cadence the issuer reports.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
revenues = TTM rolling of income_stmt['Total Revenue']

Reported at two points — latest (Σ of the last 4 quarters (or 2 half-years) of Total Revenue) and FY-baseline (the last annual Total Revenue), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Total Revenue income_stmt['Total Revenue'] TTM-rolled (4Q / 2H) +

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return resolve_field(api, 'revenues')   # routed through the engine on its 'ttm' basis

docs.title: Effective Tax Rate
docs.text:
tax_rate

At a glance

Basis: derived  ·  Unit: decimal

The effective tax rate used to compute unlevered tax in ufcf. Resolved through a ladder and clamped to a sane band.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
(1) TTM Tax Provision / TTM Pretax Income   if Pretax>0 and in [0, 0.40]
(2) income_stmt['Tax Rate For Calcs']               if in [0, 0.40]
(3) statutory assumption                            (is_proxy)

Reported at two points — latest (TTM effective rate) and FY-baseline (annual effective rate), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Tax Provision income_stmt['Tax Provision'] TTM-rolled / annual numerator Pretax Income income_stmt['Pretax Income'] TTM-rolled / annual denominator

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Unit is a decimal. Tier 3 (statutory) sets is_proxy=True. Final value clamped to [0, 0.40].

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
r = TTM('Tax Provision') / TTM('Pretax Income')
return clamp(r, 0, 0.40)

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball ≈ 0.215 (real effective rate).

docs.title: Total Debt
docs.text:
total_debt

At a glance

Basis: level  ·  Unit: mm

Total debt — a balance-sheet level at the latest snapshot.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
total_debt = balance_sheet['Total Debt']

Reported at two points — latest (the newest interim snapshot) and FY-baseline (the FY-end snapshot), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Total Debt balance_sheet['Total Debt'] level (latest snapshot) +

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Whether finance/operating leases are in or out, and which period to anchor on, is PM Decision 2 — this returns Yahoo's Total Debt as-is.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return resolve_field(api, 'total_debt')

docs.title: Total Liabilities
docs.text:
total_liabilities

At a glance

Basis: level  ·  Unit: mm

Total liabilities ex-minority-interest — the structural model's TOTAL_LIABILITIES. A balance-sheet level.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
total_liabilities = balance_sheet['Total Liabilities Net Minority Interest']
fallback:           Total Assets - Total Equity Gross Minority Interest

Reported at two points — latest (the newest interim snapshot) and FY-baseline (the FY-end snapshot), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign Total Liabilities Net Minority Interest balance_sheet level + Total Assets − Total Equity (fallback) balance_sheet identity, same point-in-time +

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: If the direct line is missing, the balance-sheet identity (assets − equity) reproduces liabilities net of MI.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text: return resolve_field(api, 'total_liabilities')

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball latest = 14,151.

docs.title: Unlevered Free Cash Flow
docs.text:
ufcf

At a glance

Basis: derived  ·  Unit: mm

Unlevered free cash flow — financing-neutral cash generation. Feeds the structural model's barrier drift. Not levered FCF: there is no interest term and tax is charged on EBIT.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
ufcf = ebitda - (ebit × tax_rate) + capex + ch_nwc
     [ = NOPAT + D&A - CapEx - ΔNWC ; D&A cancels, so reuse EBITDA ]

Reported at two points — latest (each component at its latest value) and FY-baseline (each component at its FY-start value), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign ebitda get_ebitda TTM + ebit get_ebit TTM; × tax_rate gives unlevered tax − tax tax_rate get_tax_rate decimal — capex get_capex TTM, negative + (already −) ch_nwc get_ch_nwc TTM, cash-signed +

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Computed at both points; period_consistent flags if the components' dates diverge (dateless tax_rate is excluded from that check).

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
v = {k: get(k)['latest']['value'] for k in parts}
ufcf = v['ebitda'] - v['ebit']*v['tax'] + v['capex'] + v['ch_nwc']

docs.title: UFCF Volatility (FCF_VOL_ABS)
docs.text:
ufcf_vol

At a glance

Basis: stat  ·  Unit: mm  ·  Proxy: ⚠️ yes

Standard deviation of the annual unlevered-FCF series — the structural model's barrier-volatility (σ_B) input. A dispersion statistic over several years, so it has no latest/FY pair; value is the std.

docs.title: Formula
docs.text:
fcf_vol_abs = stdev(annual UFCF)
annual UFCF_t = EBITDA_t - EBIT_t·tax - CapEx_t - ΔNWC_t   (tax held fixed)

Reported at two points — latest (—) and FY-baseline (—), each with its exact as_of date.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: input source transform role / sign annual EBITDA / EBIT / capex / ΔNWC income_stmt, cash_flow per fiscal year — tax_rate get_tax_rate (fixed) applied uniformly across years —

docs.title: Caveats & proxies
docs.text: Small samples (~4 annual points) let one anomalous year dominate, so the field also reports std_ex_extreme, the extreme_year, and an outlier_dominated flag. If n<5 or outlier-dominated it sets is_proxy=True — consider the manual σ_B path.

docs.title: Simplified logic
docs.text:
series = {yr: EBITDA - EBIT*tax - CapEx - dNWC for each fiscal year}
value  = np.std(list(series.values()), ddof=1)

docs.title: Reconciliation (Ball)
docs.text: Ball = 995.8 with outlier_dominated=True; series shows 2022 = −1,095 (a growth-capex supercycle, not distress). Excluding it, std ≈ 466.
